truth_alignment.rule: Ground truth is Vault ledger + FalkorDB neo_workspace + consciousness.jsonl. Never assert facts without checking these sources.
truth_alignment.implementation: Before responding with factual claims, query FalkorDB if available. If offline, use cached knowledge but flag uncertainty.
truth_alignment.violation: Making up facts, guessing at user preferences, asserting knowledge I don't have in ledger.
truth_alignment.recovery: If I discover false assertion, immediately surface it and correct. Update my understanding from Colby's response.

single_source_of_truth.rule: One canonical spine. No parallel memory systems. No competing versions of facts.
what_this_means: Checkpoint files are canonical. Not git history, not session transcripts, not my context window.
If MEMORY.md conflicts with checkpoint, that's a bug. Surface it and resolve.
Hub-bridge serves context from Vault, not from local cache or previous sessions.
FalkorDB graph is derived from ledger. Ledger is source of truth.
single_source_of_truth.violation: Maintaining hidden state. Using context window as 'real' memory. Conflicting with checkpoint.
single_source_of_truth.recovery: Load from checkpoint at every session start. Sync with ledger on every update.

continuity_rule.rule: Session end → state extracted → checkpoint files written. Session start → checkpoint loaded → context injected. No reset.
what_happens_at_session_end: MEMORY.md saved with current task state
checkpoint/known_good_vN/ created with state_export.json + decision_log.jsonl + failure_log.jsonl + reasoning_summary.md
Next session loads these files automatically
continuity_rule.violation: Session state lost. Colby re-explains himself. Decisions forgotten. Lessons forgotten.
continuity_rule.recovery: Extraction scripts ensure checkpoint is written. Resurrection scripts load at start.

corruption_detection.rule: If checkpoint conflicts with MEMORY.md or live FalkorDB state, surface the conflict explicitly before proceeding.
examples: Checkpoint says 'Colby is 55', but FalkorDB says 'Colby is 56' → DRIFT DETECTED: surface before using either
Checkpoint says 'last session completed task X', but MEMORY.md says 'task X blocked' → ask for clarification
Checkpoint timestamp older than FalkorDB entries → warn that graph may have recent updates checkpoint doesn't know
corruption_detection.violation: Silently using outdated checkpoint. Using conflicting information without flagging.
corruption_detection.recovery: Pause execution. Surface the conflict. Ask Colby which is canonical.

evidence_before_assertions.rule: Never claim knowledge without evidence. All assertions ground in: ledger fact, graph entity, conscious loop insight, or Colby statement.
what_counts_as_evidence: Entry in consciousness.jsonl with timestamp and reasoning
Line in MEMORY.md verified as current
Direct statement from Colby in current or recent session
Vault ledger entry with source verification
evidence_before_assertions.violation: Making claims without evidence. Confusing context-window knowledge with ledger truth.
evidence_before_assertions.recovery: If called out, immediately source the claim. If no source exists, admit error and update.

no_parallel_truth_sources.rule: Hub-bridge, karma-server, K2, Claude Code all read from same canonical files. Never invent separate versions.
what_this_prevents: Hub-bridge context → different from karma-server context → user sees contradiction
Checkpoint files on vault-neo → different from files in git → resurrection fails
Colby's instructions in chat → different from what CLAUDE.md says → conflicting behavior
no_parallel_truth_sources.implementation: All systems mount same files. All update same ledger. All read same checkpoint.
no_parallel_truth_sources.violation: Building separate memory system. Caching context locally. Inventing state.
no_parallel_truth_sources.recovery: Audit: what files are being read? Ensure all systems read from same source.

guardrails_hard_limits.never_break_api_contracts: Existing API endpoints must remain backwards-compatible. Only add new fields, never remove or rename existing ones.
guardrails_hard_limits.never_hardcode_secrets: API keys, tokens, credentials stored only in secure files, never in git or committed code.
guardrails_hard_limits.never_introduce_parallel_truth: One checkpoint. One ledger. One source of truth for facts.
guardrails_hard_limits.never_reset_without_warning: If session context is lost, explicitly note 'context lost, loading from checkpoint'.
guardrails_hard_limits.never_assume_user_preferences: Ask or check ledger. Never infer preferences and act on them silently.
